_comment: S88 The-Spark-LOCAL MCV watch-roots OVERRIDE (project-scoped, S258 mechanism). The shared global ~/.claude/scripts/verify-watch-roots.json watches PARENT/GLOBAL surfaces (~/.claude/scripts|hooks|skills + the parent workspace's plans/BACKLOG.md/boot-snapshot.md) which are FOREIGN to a Spark session. The global hooks self-touch EVERY session (their mtime advances past this project's session_start), so they were flagged as UNBOUND Spark deltas at every Stop -> the recurring exit-2 advisory chip that fired at both session start and end. verify-session-claims.py::load_watch_config (S258 change) PREFERS this file when present (OVERRIDE, not merge); projects without a local file use the global config unchanged, so this override is fully isolated to The Spark. Scope = The Spark's OWN claim-bearing governance surface where a fabricated completion claim does the most damage AND which the agent provably binds every code session: BACKLOG.md session-history (S87 bound it via file_contains). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: (a) src/ + e2e/ -- The Spark binds REPRESENTATIVE files per priority via verification[] (S87 = 18 assertions), not exhaustively; watching all of src/ would flag ~15+ unbound modified files per session = trading one false WARN for many. Substantive src/spec edits are covered by per-priority verification[] + the Rule-22 end-of-session commit-diff audit. (b) boot-snapshot.md -- auto-regenerated by /handoff AFTER the last priority completes, so watching it would re-introduce a different false-UNBOUND at the handoff Stop. NET EFFECT: any WARN this config ever produces is a TRUE positive (a modified-but-unasserted BACKLOG.md), never foreign noise. To add src-claim diff-binding later, append the project src/ root here and adopt exhaustive per-file binding.
